text.title: Dependency-Graph Reasoning Output Contract
text.summary: The methodology produces a graph file (source of truth), a derived JSON form (for queries), and a weekly risk report. All three are committed.

list.item: program-graph.mmd (or .dot / .json) — source of truth, human-editable.
program-graph.json — derived from source; consumed by query scripts.
weekly-risk-report-yyyy-Www.md — outputs of the three standard queries plus narrative.
graph-change-log.md — append-only record of merged PRs against the graph.
id — stable, team-prefixed (TA_, TB_, TC_, M_ for milestones).
team — owning team.
title — short.
tracker_link — Jira / Linear / GitHub Project URL.
target_date — ISO date or week.
status — planned / in-progress / at-risk / done / blocked.
from, to — node IDs.
type — FS / SS / FF / SF / HANDOFF.
artifact — required if type=HANDOFF.
discovered_date — ISO date.
Stale-graph alert (if > 7 days since last refresh).
Blocked-by-status table.
At-risk-if-slip-1-week scenarios (top 3 at-risk nodes + downstream impact).
Longest-path-to-milestone per upcoming cross-team milestone.
Recommended actions (escalation, scope-cut, re-plan).

antipattern.description: Graph diagram in a tool (Miro / Lucidchart) without a corresponding committed source file.
antipattern.reason: Violates r2. Source of truth is unrecoverable.

antipattern.description: Edges without types — "A depends on B" with no FS / SS / HANDOFF distinction.
Direct push to the graph file without PR review.
antipattern.reason: Violates r5. Hidden dependencies merged silently.

antipattern.description: Weekly report skipped or generated from a stale graph.
antipattern.reason: Violates r3 + r4. PM cannot answer risk questions defensibly.

list.item: Graph source file present and validated (no cycles, all nodes prefixed, all edges typed).
JSON form rebuilt this week.
Three standard queries ran; outputs in the weekly report.
Graph-change-log updated for all merged changes.
No nodes with status = "blocked" without a linked tracker URL.
